_note: DOUBT-PROBE (continuous dynamics = a 7th primitive?): can a PURE RULE over neighbor edges reproduce continuous heat diffusion WITH conservation, using zero new machinery? 8 cells in a border-chain, cell 0 starts hot. Rule: food += k*(edge_mean(border,food) - food) = the discretized heat equation. Watch: mean food (should stay ~0.125 = CONSERVATION), and hold.field = MIN cell heat via rollup (should rise 0 -> 0.125 as the front reaches the coldest cell = DIFFUSION). If both happen, continuous field evolution is expressible as rules-over-neighbors, no 7th prim.
